q: Artificial satellites going around the earth at a constant height is an example of ___.
Which of the following is defined as a body that does not deform or change shape?
In rotational motion, all particles of a rigid body move about a ___.
Angular acceleration is constant when the ___ changes at a constant rate.
The kinematic equation $\omega^2 = \omega_0^2 + 2 \alpha \theta$ is analogous to which linear equation?
The symbol $\alpha$ represents ___.
The rate of change of angular velocity with respect to time is called ___.
All particles of an object rotating around an axis move in a path that is ___.
The centre of rotation for all particles of a rotating rigid body lies on the ___.
The relation between arc length $s$, radius $r$, and angular displacement $\theta$ is ___.
For a rotating rigid body, the instantaneous angular velocity $\omega$ is ___ for all particles.
The linear velocity $v$ of a particle is also called ___ velocity.
The direction of linear velocity is always ___ to the circular path.
Tangential acceleration $a_T$ is a measure of how fast the ___ changes.
The direction of the angular velocity vector is determined by the ___.
If the angular velocity is increasing, the angular acceleration vector $\vec{\alpha}$ points ___ the angular velocity vector $\vec{\omega}$.
Uniform circular motion is characterized by moving in a circle at a constant ___.
In uniform circular motion, acceleration is caused by a change in ___.
The centripetal acceleration vector always points toward the ___.
Centripetal acceleration $a_c$ is also known as ___ acceleration.
The formula for centripetal acceleration in terms of angular velocity is ___.
In uniform circular motion, the centripetal acceleration and linear velocity are ___.
Nonuniform circular motion involves moving in a circular path with a ___.
In nonuniform circular motion, the resultant acceleration is the vector sum of ___.
Which acceleration is present in both uniform and nonuniform circular motion?
The magnitude of the resultant acceleration $a$ is calculated using the formula ___.
